patch.insert_text: - Start hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning.
added_claims: Started hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning

reasons: HCTZ 25 mg oral tablet every morning is supported by the transcript span and FHIR MedicationRequest (RxNorm 310798).
Note's hypertension plan only mentions lifestyle education and BP recheck; no antihypertensive medication was previously listed.
Medication start for essential hypertension correctly belongs in Assessment and Plan.

patch.insert_text: - Start hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning with breakfast.
added_claims: Started hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning with breakfast

reasons: Core claim (hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning) is supported by both the transcript span and the FHIR MedicationRequest.
The added detail 'with breakfast' is not present in the visible evidence (transcript says only 'every morning'; FHIR dosage is truncated), so that portion is unsupported/invented.
No existing medication for hypertension is documented, so the insertion is non-redundant.
Assessment and Plan is the correct SOAP section for a new medication order; it belongs with the Essential hypertension problem (reasonReference confirms).
